response.justification: Bogdan holds genuine Staff-level credentials and his current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, which maps well to an AI-native engineering environment. His time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic demonstrates credible high-throughput distributed systems work at scale. The critical blocker is Python: it is listed as a must-have and is entirely absent from both his skills section and every role description, with his stack skewing heavily toward TypeScript, DLang, and Java. Additionally, the resume offers no evidence of stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination, or on-call incident-response ownership, and his current AI-focused role began in April 2025, leaving the depth of that experience unverifiable.
strengths: Current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq directly matches the AI-native engineering baseline the role expects
Proven distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Long polyglot career with Staff-level titles at multiple companies and a consistent thread of technical leadership and developer tooling
concerns: Python is a stated must-have and is conspicuously absent from every skills list and role description across a 17-year career
No evidence of stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination, or operational ownership such as on-call rotations or incident response
Current AI-infrastructure role started April 2025 — only weeks of tenure — making depth of agentic-systems experience difficult to assess
